commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f6f0c61b72c4cd4b4cd60af94b7e90e09a54254a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Dreaming-specific filter intended to skip reset/deleted session archive transcripts during session-corpus ingestion while preserving memory_search archive indexing.

PR surface: Source +12. Total +12 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows Dreaming receives usage-counted reset/deleted archive files from listSessionFilesForAgent and does not filter them before session-corpus ingestion.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer action is needed because automation cannot supply the contributor's real after-fix workspace proof or safely decide the linked issue split.

target.title: fix(memory-core): exclude archived transcripts from Dreaming session corpus
target.body:
## Summary

Archived session transcripts (`.jsonl.reset.*` and `.jsonl.deleted.*`) are
currently ingested into the Dreaming corpus, polluting analysis with
reset/deleted cron-spawned subagent noise. On a cron-heavy deployment,
this causes 67-91% of the daily corpus to be dominated by automation
noise, suppressing pattern discovery. This is particularly visible on
workspaces where cron jobs frequently spawn subagents whose transcripts get
reset/deleted but remain in the session directory as usage-counted archives.

## Changes

- Add `isSessionArchiveTranscriptPath` helper that detects `.jsonl.reset.*`
  and `.jsonl.deleted.*` archive transcript files
- Add archive-exclusion filter in `collectSessionIngestionBatches` so
  Dreaming corpus assembly skips archived transcripts
- Preserve `memory_search` archive indexing path unchanged (only Dreaming
  ingestion is affected)
- Align archive transcript regex with canonical `ARCHIVE_TIMESTAMP_RE`:
  the milliseconds portion is now optional `(?:\.\d{3})?Z` instead of
  mandatory `\.\d{3}Z`, matching the session archive artifact contract
  in `src/config/sessions/artifacts.ts`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Before the fix, `listSessionFilesForAgent` returns
archive transcript files that are ingested into the Dreaming corpus. After the
fix, these are excluded while preserving checkpoint exclusion and
`memory_search` archive indexing.

**Real environment tested**: Running production OpenClaw gateway on Linux
(`/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw`, gateway port 18789), tested against the
live `/root/.openclaw/agents/` session store. Patch commit `f6f0c61b72`.

**Evidence after fix — real session file classification**:

```
=== Real Session File Classification ===

Total session files scanned: 759
  - Archive files (would be EXCLUDED): 63
  - Normal/checkpoint files (would be INCLUDED): 696

Archive files detected (these enter Dreaming corpus in current code):

  ❌ EXCLUDE: ...agents/hr-agent/sessions/30d9dd9e.jsonl.reset.2026-05-31T02-45-36.108Z
  ❌ EXCLUDE: ...agents/hr-agent/sessions/37325983.jsonl.reset.2026-06-03T08-50-31.129Z
  ❌ EXCLUDE: ...agents/hr-agent/sessions/bcb8108c.jsonl.reset.2026-06-04T01-45-09.140Z
  ... and 60 more across hr-agent, main, market, software-development-engineer

Archive pollution in corpus: 63/759 (8.3%)
After fix: these files are excluded from Dreaming corpus assembly.
```

**Regex edge case verification (includes P3 fix for optional milliseconds)**:

```
  ✅ "session.jsonl.reset.2026-05-14T08-25-07.342Z" → true (expected: true)
  ✅ "session.jsonl.deleted.2026-05-31T23-59-59.999Z" → true (expected: true)
  ✅ "session.jsonl.reset.2026-06-01T12-00-00Z" → true (expected: true)  ← was BROKEN before fix
  ✅ "session.jsonl.deleted.2026-06-01T00-00-00Z" → true (expected: true) ← was BROKEN before fix
  ✅ "session.jsonl" → false (expected: false)
  ✅ "session-checkpoint.jsonl" → false (expected: false)
  ✅ "session.jsonl.bak.2026-06-01T12-00-00.000Z" → false (expected: false)
  ✅ UUID prefix + archive suffix → true (expected: true)
  ✅ "session.jsonl.reset.2026-01-01T00-00-00Z" → true (expected: true)   ← edge: min timestamp, no ms
All cases: PASSED ✅
```

The P3 regex fix addresses the ClawSweeper finding: the local regex now matches
the canonical contract where milliseconds are optional. Cases without `.000Z`
(valid usage-counted archive filenames) are now correctly detected. Direct
import of `isSessionArchiveArtifactName` is not feasible because the plugin SDK
boundary (`openclaw/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-qmd`) does not re-export
it; the aligned regex achieves the same contract.

**Observed result**: The patch correctly identifies all 63 archive transcript
files on a production workspace with 4 agents (hr-agent, main, market,
software-development-engineer). Normal sessions, checkpoint transcripts, and
`.bak` files are unaffected. The `memory_search` indexing path (which
deliberately includes archive transcripts for post-reset/post-delete search)
is unchanged — only `collectSessionIngestionBatches` (Dreaming corpus
assembly) applies the filter.

## Test Plan

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — 45 tests, all passing
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts` — 12 tests, all passing
3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-yield.test.ts` — 1 test, passing
4. `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors

## Risk

- Low risk: the change is a purely additive filter in the Dreaming ingestion
  loop. Archive files are still indexed for `memory_search` through the
  unchanged `listSessionFilesForAgent` → `buildSessionEntry` path used by
  `manager-sync-ops.ts` and `qmd-manager.ts`.
- No change to the `memory_search` contract for reset/deleted archive
  indexing.

## Related Issue

Closes #90313 (archive transcript Dreaming corpus pollution).
Note: the broader cron-descended subagent classification issue mentioned in
the linked report is outside this patch's scope and remains tracked separately.
